name: Almost-no-Fat Grand Marnier Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Non-fat egg substitute (equivalent to 2 eggs)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle gelatin over Grand Marnier and let stand until softened.
2. Combine 2 cups milk, non-fat dry milk, vanilla, and orange zest in a saucepan.
3. Heat to simmer. Add a small amount of hot mixture to egg substitute and return all to the pan.
4. Heat and stir until slightly thickened. Stir in gelatin mixture until dissolved.
5. Stir in remaining milk. Let cool to room temperature.
6. Stir in vanilla. Process in ice cream maker according to manufacturer's directions.
7. To serve, spoon ice cream into orange shells and garnish with orange zest if desired.
8. For six orange shells, cut three medium oranges in half crosswise. Gently juice the oranges, being careful not to damage the shells. Reserve the juice for another use. Scoop any remaining membrane from the inner shells. Chill until serving time.
9. Makes 6 servings.
10. Each serving contains about: 305 calories; 368 milligrams sodium; 12 milligrams cholesterol; 0 fat; 44 grams carbohydrates; 25 grams protein; 0 fiber.
